title: Audio Books Page on my Website Tour - Stoned

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a tour of the free audio history books section on his website, explaining how he organizes 1,159 books across 15 sections and 9 world regions, with color coding, sorting by length, and metadata like chapter counts, page counts, and listening hours. He highlights the Mahabharata as the longest book at 213+ hours and describes his layout plans.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: dividing by size is a good way to organize
switching to horizontal block sections because seeing the book cover in one glance is better
his website is the best and possibly only place to download the computer-voiced version
fun to pair long audiobooks with long rides

claims.subject: free audio history book section
claims.claim: These are all of the three, audio history books.

claims.subject: USA page
claims.claim: The USA page has 331 books.

claims.subject: religion section
claims.claim: There are 46 books in the religion section.

claims.subject: sections
claims.claim: There are different 15 sections, having to do with America.

claims.subject: sections
claims.claim: There are like 78 different sections here.

claims.subject: world sections
claims.claim: There are nine different parts of the world represented in the sections.

claims.subject: America section
claims.claim: America's 331 books make it large enough to get its own section.

claims.subject: Italy section
claims.claim: Italy got its own section.

claims.subject: organization method
claims.claim: Dividing the books based on size is a good way to organize them.

claims.subject: organization method
claims.claim: The sections were built when I had my Windows computer and had memory issues.

claims.subject: color coding
claims.claim: I'm in the process of color coding things.

claims.subject: organization dimensions
claims.claim: There are three different dimensions of organizing the books.

claims.subject: sections
claims.claim: One dimension shows the 15 different sections.

claims.subject: titles
claims.claim: Another dimension shows the titles of the books and all the different sections.

claims.subject: chapters
claims.claim: A third dimension shows the title of the books with the title of all the chapters inside.

claims.subject: free audio history books section
claims.claim: I like to call my free audio history books section 'the book of books.'

claims.subject: religion novels
claims.claim: Here is religion novels.

claims.subject: philosophy
claims.claim: Philosophy goes down this far.

claims.subject: book organization
claims.claim: Right now I have books organized vertically.

claims.subject: book organization
claims.claim: I'm going to have them after this horizontal and block sections.

claims.subject: book organization
claims.claim: I had it vertically because I liked having the titles of all the books next to each other.

claims.subject: book organization
claims.claim: I think being able to look at the book as one minute is better, so having that in a block is preferable.

claims.subject: audio history book section
claims.claim: Here's my audio history book section.

claims.subject: time page
claims.claim: The time while it's been a history time while, you know, that's a time page.

claims.subject: book metrics
claims.claim: This means how many books it is.

claims.subject: book metrics
claims.claim: This means how many chapters it has.

claims.subject: book metrics
claims.claim: This means how many pages the book has.

claims.subject: book metrics
claims.claim: This means how many hours it is to read it.

claims.subject: reading time
claims.claim: 72 hours 55 minutes is also called three days and 55 hours.

claims.subject: pairing with rides
claims.claim: It's fun to pair this with a ride, like a 72-hour long ride.

claims.subject: book listing
claims.claim: This is all of the different books listed, all 1159 books that I have.

claims.subject: book sources
claims.claim: The ones with the red background are from the green box.

claims.subject: book sources
claims.claim: The ones with the black background are from Amazon.

claims.subject: book organization
claims.claim: This is organized from the longest biggest book to the smallest.

claims.subject: Mahabharata
claims.claim: The Mahabharata is the biggest book that you can have it read to you.

claims.subject: Mahabharata
claims.claim: You can download the Mahabharata off my website.

claims.subject: Mahabharata
claims.claim: The computer voice is reading it to you, one of very good voice.

claims.subject: Mahabharata
claims.claim: The Mahabharata is 12,829 pages.

claims.subject: Mahabharata
claims.claim: The Mahabharata was compiled in the 380s from historical stories from before that.

claims.subject: Mahabharata
claims.claim: The Mahabharata is 213 hours 49 minutes long, which is eight days to 21 hours.

claims.subject: non-fiction collection
claims.claim: Next is non-fiction collection by various things.

claims.subject: world-class greatest books
claims.claim: World-class greatest books are next after that.

claims.subject: book organization
claims.claim: The first huge column is 20 books.

claims.subject: book organization
claims.claim: The first 20 books have over 1,730 pages.

claims.subject: book organization
claims.claim: The first hundred books have over 900 pages.

claims.subject: color coding
claims.claim: These colors are having to do with what part of the world they are from.

claims.claim: I don't have the key organized yet.

claims.claim: This is America.

claims.claim: That's my great idea of history books.
